blurb:
At six years old, Karla has figured out exactly who she is, and who she is happens to be a very good girl. She's a petite German Shepherd with a gentle personality and, according to the volunteers, a coat that occasionally has its own agenda. She's going to need someone willing to take her for the occasional grooming run. That's a pretty easy ask for a dog this sweet.

She gets along with other dogs, has experience with kids, and keeps her energy at a steady, manageable pace. She's not going to tear through your house. She's more the type to settle in beside you and just be present. That kind of calm is genuinely hard to find in a shelter dog.

One thing worth knowing: Karla tested positive for heartworm. It sounds scarier than it is. Lewisville Animal Services will cover her full treatment, so you're not walking into a vet bill surprise. She's also fully vaccinated, spayed, microchipped, and flea-treated. She just needs someone to get her through treatment and into a home where she can finally stay.

She's at Lewisville Animal Services on College Parkway. Walk in during visiting hours to meet her, or call ahead if you have questions about the heartworm protocol.
scoring_notes:
• Heartworm-positive senior Shepherd with fully covered treatment is the highest-stakes, highest-reward story in this set — ideal newsletter centerpiece
• Her exceptionally low intake ID number signals the longest shelter stay of any candidate, making featured placement genuinely urgent
• Calm energy, kid experience, and dog compatibility make her the most broadly adoptable dog in the group despite the medical flag

blurb:
Lexington goes by Wicket at home, which should tell you something about his personality right away. He's a three-year-old Jack Russell-Rat Terrier mix, about 17 pounds, and he is fully convinced he should be sitting in your lap at all times. He will try. He will succeed.

His foster describes him as a total cuddler with a silly streak and a great balance of playful and chill. He hasn't touched the furniture (a genuine win), loves people, and will position himself as close to you as physically possible the moment you sit down. He was adopted for about eight months before coming back to Mostly Mutts, and it just came down to a bad fit with the cats in that home. Nothing more complicated than that.

Lexington does have a strong prey drive, so no cats. He can warm up to other dogs with a slow, proper intro, and he's done well with mellow pups in his foster home. He's more comfortable with older kids who give him his space, 10 and up at minimum. He's a small dog with a big personality and he's going to be deeply loyal to whoever takes him home.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. When you reach out, ask about Lexington (not Wicket) since that's the name in his records.
shelter_hours: See mostlymutts.org for current hours and appointment info
scoring_notes:
• Returned after eight months through no behavioral fault of his own — a genuinely urgent second-chance story that motivates action
• Dual identity as Lexington and 'Wicket' plus the lap-obsessed cuddler detail gives the newsletter a personality-driven, immediately lovable hook
• Prior adoption return combined with cat and multi-dog restrictions suggests extended shelter exposure, making him the highest-priority feature candidate

blurb:
Victor is a five-year-old Bulldog mix, about 44 pounds, and his whole body wags when he's happy. Not just the tail. The whole thing. The volunteers at Mostly Mutts call him a super sweet boy, and he went through the prison training program, so he's had real, consistent work put into him.

He's got a big personality and a lot of love to give. He's calm and angelic at bath time, great with one-on-one attention, and the kind of dog who just wants to be with his person. He can get mouthy when he's excited, which has occasionally turned into an accidental skin grab, but he redirects easily and the trainers will show you exactly what works for him.

He'd do best as your only dog since he hasn't clicked with other dogs. Kids 15 and up are a better fit given his strength and excitement level around younger ones. If that sounds like your house, he's got a lot to offer. Mostly Mutts is in Marietta. Reach out through their website or give them a call to meet Victor.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• 'Whole body wags' is a quotable, shareable detail that gives a newsletter blurb immediate warmth and viral potential
• Prison training program backstory paired with ongoing mouthiness redirects well as a 'diamond in the rough' narrative
• Only-dog requirement plus age restriction for kids narrows the pool considerably, suggesting he has been waiting and needs a targeted push
